senses.definition: a long board we stand or lie on to ride waves

examples.x: They rented surfboards and wetsuits for their surfing lesson, eager to learn how to ride the waves.
The surfboard was custom-made to fit his height, weight, and surfing style.
He waxed his surfboard and headed to the beach to catch some waves.
She enjoys surfing and spends her weekends riding her surfboard along the coastline.

senses.definition: a small board with two sets of wheels we stand on to move around by pushing one foot down

examples.x: He enjoys riding his skateboard around the neighborhood, doing tricks and jumps at the local skate park.
The local skate shop offers a wide selection of skateboards, trucks, wheels, and accessories for enthusiasts of all levels.
He customized his skateboard with colorful grip tape and stickers to reflect his personal style.
She landed a kickflip on her skateboard after practicing for weeks, feeling a sense of accomplishment.
He used his skateboard as his primary mode of transportation, zipping through traffic and navigating busy streets with ease.

senses.definition: a type of water sport equipment resembling a small surfboard, designed for riding on water's surface while being towed behind a motorboat

examples.x: He performed impressive flips and jumps on his wakeboard during the competition.
Every wakeboarder wears a life jacket for safety while riding the wakeboard.
She enjoyed the thrill of gliding across the water on her wakeboard.
The wakeboard's bindings provided ankle support for stability during tricks.

senses.definition: a surfboard with a mast and sail for wind-powered water sports like windsurfing

examples.x: The beach was crowded with colorful sailboards.
Learning to balance on a sailboard takes practice.
Can you help me carry the sailboard to the water?
Renting a sailboard is a great way to try a new water sport.
We packed sunscreen, towels, and the sailboard for our beach trip.

senses.definition: a short, buoyant board ridden lying down on waves, used in bodyboarding

examples.x: Watch out for the waves, hold onto your bodyboard!
Hold your breath when diving under a wave on your bodyboard.
I rented a bodyboard for some fun in the waves.
With a bodyboard, you can catch waves closer to the shore.
We rented bodyboards and fins for a fun day at the beach.

senses.definition: a long skateboard designed for cruising, carving, and downhill racing

examples.x: She gripped the longboard and pushed off, gaining speed.
We can customize the longboard with different wheels and trucks.
I'm saving up to buy a new longboard.
Can you teach me how to ollie on my longboard?

senses.definition: a long, stable platform with a sail that harnesses wind power to propel you across the water in windsurfing or boardsailing

examples.x: I rented a windsurf board for the afternoon.
Be careful not to fall off the windsurf board!
Bigger windsurf board for calmer winds, smaller for speed.
I dream of owning my own windsurf board someday.
Fell off the windsurf board, waves were rough!

senses.definition: a board ridden on water, propelled by a large kite that the rider controls

examples.x: We rented kiteboards and took lessons at the beach.
I need to buy new fins for my kiteboard.
Can you help me inflate the kiteboard?
Where can we buy a good quality kiteboard?
Hold on tight to the kiteboard handle!

senses.definition: a small, flat board used for gliding over the water's surface in sports like skimboarding and surfing

examples.x: She practiced her balance on the skimboard every day.
The skimboard slipped smoothly over the shallow water.
They bought a new skimboard for their beach vacation.
He waxed his skimboard to reduce friction.
A broken skimboard lay abandoned by the shore.

senses.definition: a large, buoyant board used for stand-up paddling, a water sport where individuals stand and paddle on bodies of water

examples.x: He took his stand-up paddle board to the lake for a relaxing paddle.
She loves the freedom of exploring the ocean on her stand-up paddle board.
They rented stand-up paddle board for their vacation in Hawaii.

senses.definition: long, flat board used for standing and paddling in sports like stand-up paddleboarding and surfing

examples.x: We rented a paddleboard at the beach.
He paddled across the lake on his paddleboard.
She bought a new paddleboard for her summer vacation.
He carried his paddleboard to the shore.
She fell off her paddleboard but quickly got back on.

senses.definition: either of a pair of long thin objects worn on our feet to make us move faster over the snow

examples.x: She strapped on her skis and glided gracefully down the mountain slope.
The skis were waxed and tuned to ensure optimal performance on the snow.
He carefully adjusted the bindings on his skis to ensure a secure fit before hitting the slopes.
The ski resort offers rentals for skis, boots, and poles for those who don't have their own equipment.

senses.definition: a type of ski designed for use in deep, soft snow, typically used in alpine skiing and backcountry skiing

examples.x: I just bought a new pair of powder skis for the upcoming ski trip.
Powder skis have a wider base to help you float on deep snow.
She prefers powder skis when skiing off-piste.
The ski shop recommended powder skis for the backcountry adventure.
Powder skis make a significant difference in deep snow conditions.

senses.definition: a versatile type of ski designed for use on various terrains and snow conditions, commonly used in alpine skiing

examples.x: She bought a new pair of all-mountain skis for her ski trip.
All-mountain skis are perfect for both groomed runs and powder.
She tested the all-mountain skis on both icy and soft snow.
All-mountain skis are ideal for exploring different parts of the mountain.
The instructor advised using all-mountain skis for the lesson.

senses.definition: a type of ski used in freestyle skiing events

examples.x: He bought a new freestyle ski for the competition.
She waxed her freestyle ski before hitting the slopes.
The lightweight freestyle ski helps him perform jumps easily.
Her freestyle ski is designed for better tricks in the park.
He prefers using a freestyle ski for its flexibility.

senses.definition: a type of ski specifically designed for competitive alpine skiing and ski racing

examples.x: She bought a new pair of race skis for the upcoming competition.
He prefers using race skis on the steeper slopes.
Race skis are built for speed and precision.
His race skis cut smoothly through the snow.
Race skis require regular maintenance for optimal performance.
